title: worker: async/Promise-returning port silently mis-graded as DataCloneError
description: worker.ts:146 calls 'const value = fn(...inputArgs)' synchronously. If the port is async or returns a Promise, structured-clone of the Promise throws DataCloneError on postMessage. The grader sees an unhelpful 'could not be cloned' error instead of the real 'port violates sync contract' diagnosis. Docstring L13-14 asserts sync contract but does not enforce it. Major because sonnet L3 frequently emits async ports — the failure mode disguises itself as a serialization bug, masking the real defect. Fix: detect thenable post-call (typeof value.then === 'function') and throw 'port returned a Promise; sync contract violated' inside the try block.
close_reason: Fixed in Step 4 triage: 3-file edit pass, tsc clean. See Step 5 followup mpfr-ts-637.

title: Step 5 followup: verify partial harness files + complete runner.ts
description: Step 5 (mpfr-ts-odi.5) was stopped mid-stream due to session-end. eval/harness/value_codec.ts, ast_check.ts, worker.ts exist; runner.ts does not. Next agent: (1) Read all three for senior-TS quality issues; (2) verify they align with the Step 5 brief (esp. value_codec ExpectedOutput shape, worker keep-alive pattern, ast_check Cyrillic detection); (3) write runner.ts per spec; (4) execute the full acceptance suite (stub correct port pass, broken port fail, infloop terminates fast, schema violation rejected, NaN equality OK). Only close mpfr-ts-odi.5 after acceptance is GREEN.
close_reason: Step 5 complete: harness skeleton (value_codec, ast_check, worker, runner) + RED→GREEN acceptance suite, 5/5 scenarios passing, tsc clean. 9 audit bugs fixed pre-acceptance.

title: Step 10: Live sonnet L3 attempt + finalize
description: Render the mpn_add_n prompt via python3 eval/driver/ralph.py --function mpn_add_n. Save to /tmp/eval_pilot_mpn_add_n/prompt.txt. Orchestrator (parent Claude session) spawns ONE Agent(subagent_type=general-purpose, model=sonnet) with the prompt as task; agent writes /tmp/eval_pilot_mpn_add_n/port.ts. After agent returns, run bun eval/harness/runner.ts to grade; ast_check the port for core.ts import compliance; INSERT into runs table; UPDATE functions row. Accept: composite_correctness ≥ 0.95 AND ast_check passes AND state.db row reflects the run. If composite < 0.95 → file bd bug describing failure mode (Cyrillic? schema-violation? wrong carry? infinite loop?), HALT pilot per PIL.1. If composite ≥ 0.95 → write docs/worklog/001-pilot-mpn-add-n.md retrospective. PIL.5 is met only when ALL 10 pilot fns reach this state — this is just the first.
close_reason: Pilot Steps 6-10 complete for mpn_add_n. golden 152 cases, ref ports verified (correct=1.0 / broken=0.395 / gap=0.605), prompts.py + ralph.py written, sonnet L3 attempt scored 1.0/152. state.db updated. See docs/worklog/002-pilot-mpn-add-n.md. Remaining 9 pilot functions to be scaled out next.

title: Step 9: Prompt template + dry-run driver
description: Create eval/driver/prompts.py: build_prompt(run_info) emits the L3 sonnet prompt — embeds src/core.ts verbatim (Law 4 enforcement), embeds the C source from mpfr/src/ (or GMP equivalent for mpn fns), embeds spec.json contents, embeds the iteration loop instructions (write port to <path>, run bun eval/harness/runner.ts --function X --port <path> --golden <path> --output <path>, read grade.json, iterate up to 6 times, stop when composite ≥ 0.95). Create eval/driver/ralph.py with --dry-run flag that prints the rendered prompt without spawning an Agent, --function flag to pick which function. Accept: python3 eval/driver/ralph.py --function mpn_add_n --dry-run prints a complete prompt containing the words 'MPFR' (from core.ts), 'mpn_add_n' (from spec), the C signature, and the iteration loop instructions; prompt size between 5KB and 50KB.
close_reason: Pilot Steps 6-10 complete for mpn_add_n. golden 152 cases, ref ports verified (correct=1.0 / broken=0.395 / gap=0.605), prompts.py + ralph.py written, sonnet L3 attempt scored 1.0/152. state.db updated. See docs/worklog/002-pilot-mpn-add-n.md. Remaining 9 pilot functions to be scaled out next.

title: Step 8: Mutation-prove the golden
description: Run bun eval/harness/runner.ts twice: once against correct/mpn_add_n.ts, once against broken/mpn_add_n.ts. Record grade.json from both. Accept: correct composite_correctness ≥ 0.99 (ideally 1.0); broken composite_correctness ≤ 0.5; gap ≥ 0.49. If correct < 0.99 → the golden is wrong (or the reference port is wrong) — debug before continuing. If broken > 0.5 → the golden is insufficient (not catching the seeded bug) — add adversarial cases until it does. This is THE harness-soundness gate of the pilot.
close_reason: Pilot Steps 6-10 complete for mpn_add_n. golden 152 cases, ref ports verified (correct=1.0 / broken=0.395 / gap=0.605), prompts.py + ralph.py written, sonnet L3 attempt scored 1.0/152. state.db updated. See docs/worklog/002-pilot-mpn-add-n.md. Remaining 9 pilot functions to be scaled out next.

title: Step 7: Reference ports (correct + broken)
description: Create eval/reference_ports/correct/mpn_add_n.ts (hand-written, faithful, well-cited per Law 1) and eval/reference_ports/broken/mpn_add_n.ts (deliberately wrong: skip the carry propagation past index 0, or return carry as always 0n). NOTE: mpn_add_n is a substrate fn — it does NOT use the MPFR value type, so the core.ts import requirement doesn't apply. The spec is bigint[] in, {sum:bigint[], carry:bigint} out. Cite GMP §8.3 for limb endianness. Both files should be runnable via: bun eval/reference_ports/correct/mpn_add_n.ts (with a 3-line smoke import at the bottom guarded by import.meta.main). Accept: both files type-check, both export mpn_add_n with the spec signature, correct version handles single-limb and multi-limb correctly when tested by hand at REPL.
close_reason: Pilot Steps 6-10 complete for mpn_add_n. golden 152 cases, ref ports verified (correct=1.0 / broken=0.395 / gap=0.605), prompts.py + ralph.py written, sonnet L3 attempt scored 1.0/152. state.db updated. See docs/worklog/002-pilot-mpn-add-n.md. Remaining 9 pilot functions to be scaled out next.

title: Step 6: mpn_add_n spec + golden_driver + golden.jsonl
description: Create eval/functions/mpn_add_n/{spec.json, golden_driver.c}. spec.json: name=mpn_add_n, class=substrate, c_signature='mp_limb_t mpn_add_n(mp_limb_t *rp, const mp_limb_t *s1p, const mp_limb_t *s2p, mp_size_t n)', ts_signature='export function mpn_add_n(a: bigint[], b: bigint[], n: number): {sum: bigint[], carry: bigint}', deps=[], notes (limb endianness, carry semantics, n>=1 invariant). golden_driver.c: includes common.h, generates ~180 cases — happy (n=1..5, random limbs, 30 cases), edge (n=1 zero, all-ones max carry chain, single high bit, MSB of each limb, n=20 cascading carry, 40 cases), adversarial (n=50 all-0xff to force max carry, n=10 with carry-out at boundary, 20 cases), fuzz (deterministic xorshift, n in {1,2,3,5,10,20,50}, 90 cases). Each case emits {tag, inputs:{a:["123",...], b:[...], n:5}, output:{sum:[...], carry:"1"}, time_ns}. Accept: bash eval/golden_master/build.sh succeeds; bash run produces golden.jsonl with wc -l ≥ 150; all 4 tags present (grep -c '"tag":"happy"' ≥ 20, edge ≥ 30, adversarial ≥ 10, fuzz ≥ 50).
close_reason: Pilot Steps 6-10 complete for mpn_add_n. golden 152 cases, ref ports verified (correct=1.0 / broken=0.395 / gap=0.605), prompts.py + ralph.py written, sonnet L3 attempt scored 1.0/152. state.db updated. See docs/worklog/002-pilot-mpn-add-n.md. Remaining 9 pilot functions to be scaled out next.

title: Step 5: Harness skeleton (Bun worker pool)
description: Create eval/harness/{runner.ts, worker.ts, value_codec.ts, ast_check.ts} using Bun Worker API. runner.ts: parse args (--function, --port, --golden, --output), spawn worker pool of N=4, dispatch one case per message, class-tier hard wall (substrate=50ms, arithmetic=50ms, transcendental=200ms, misc=1s), worker.terminate() on timeout; aggregate composite per CLAUDE.md formula (0.6 corr + 0.2 edge + 0.2 mined; perf grade SEPARATE; composite=0 if compiled=false). worker.ts: imports port via dynamic import, calls fn with parsed args, returns {result, ms}. value_codec.ts: decodeMpfrValue(obj)->MPFR per src/core.ts; encodeForCompare(MPFR)->canonical JSON. ast_check.ts: parse port.ts string; verify 'import {...MPFR...} from "...core.ts"' present; verify no 'interface MPFR' or 'type RoundingMode' declarations; return {ok, errors}. Accept: smoke test against a stub port + stub golden (10 cases, all should pass) produces grade.json with composite=1.0; against a stub port with one wrong return produces composite<1.
notes: PARTIAL — agent stopped mid-step. Files written: eval/harness/{ast_check.ts (9KB), value_codec.ts (19KB), worker.ts (6KB)}. Files NOT written: eval/harness/runner.ts. Acceptance tests NOT run. Next session must (a) inspect the three written files for senior-TS quality, (b) write runner.ts per Step 5 brief, (c) run the FULL acceptance suite from the brief — especially the infinite-loop test. The brief is preserved as bd issue mpfr-ts-odi.5 description; re-read in full before continuing.
close_reason: Step 5 complete: harness skeleton (value_codec, ast_check, worker, runner) + RED→GREEN acceptance suite, 5/5 scenarios passing, tsc clean. 9 audit bugs fixed pre-acceptance.

title: Step 4: Golden-driver common.h
description: Create eval/golden_master/common.h adapted from ../auto-port-eval/eval/golden_master/common.h: keep xs64_seed/xs64_next/xs64_below PRNG, now_ns, jl_begin/jl_end_inputs/jl_finish JSONL helpers. ADD: jl_limb_array(f, key, limbs, n) — emits '"key":["123","456",...]' for mpn arrays (decimal strings to keep BigInt parse trivial). ADD: jl_mpfr_value(f, key, x) — emits MPFR value as JSON object (kind, sign, prec, exp_str, mant_hex via mpfr_get_str base 16). Include gmp.h and mpfr.h. Accept: 10-line test driver compiles + emits one valid JSON line that round-trips through Bun's JSON.parse.

title: Step 3: State DB schema + init
description: Create eval/driver/schema.sql with the three tables from CLAUDE.md §'State DB schema': functions, runs, cases (verbatim). Apply via sqlite3 eval/state.db < eval/driver/schema.sql. Insert one row: functions.name='mpn_add_n', class='substrate', signature='(a: bigint[], b: bigint[], n: number) => {sum: bigint[], carry: bigint}', deps='[]', status='pending', topo_rank=0. Accept: sqlite3 eval/state.db 'SELECT name, class, status FROM functions' shows mpn_add_n|substrate|pending.

title: Step 2: Locked schema src/core.ts
description: Implement src/core.ts EXACTLY as specified in CLAUDE.md §'Library coherence': MPFR interface (kind, sign, prec, exp, mant), RoundingMode string union ('RNDN'|'RNDZ'|'RNDU'|'RNDD'|'RNDA'), Ternary type (-1|0|1), Result interface ({value, ternary}), MPFRError class with code 'EPREC'|'EROUND'|'EDOMAIN'. No Bun.* or node:* imports. Accept: bun -e "import('./src/core.ts').then(m=>console.log(Object.keys(m)))" lists MPFRError; tsc --noEmit src/core.ts passes.

title: Step 1: Repo skeleton + system deps
description: Create PHASE.md, package.json (engines: bun>=1.3, node>=22), bunfig.toml, tsconfig.json, .gitignore, dir tree (src/, src/internal/{mpn,mpfr}, eval/{functions,harness,golden_master,driver,reference_ports/{correct,broken},integration,reports}, docs/{worklog,adr}). Verify libmpfr-dev, libgmp-dev, gcc, sqlite3, python3+venv, bun>=1.3, node>=22. Clone https://gitlab.inria.fr/mpfr/mpfr.git depth=1 to ./mpfr/. Accept: bun --version, gcc --version, pkg-config --cflags mpfr, ls mpfr/src/add.c all succeed.

title: Pilot: vertical slice for mpn_add_n
description: Epic tracking the 10 steps of the Pilot phase. Deliverable: harness proven sound by grading mpn_add_n end-to-end. See docs/PILOT_PLAN.md.

title: runner.ts: relative --port / --golden paths resolve against eval/harness/, not cwd
description:
When a user invokes `bun eval/harness/runner.ts --port src/internal/mpn/add_n.ts ...` from the repo root, the worker fails to import the port because `pathToUrl` resolves relative paths against `import.meta.url` (which points at `eval/harness/runner.ts`) instead of against the cwd.

Reproduce (from repo root):
  bun eval/harness/runner.ts \
    --function mpn_add_n \
    --port src/internal/mpn/add_n.ts \
    --golden eval/functions/mpn_add_n/golden.jsonl \
    --output /tmp/g.json --class substrate \
    --spec eval/functions/mpn_add_n/spec.json

Observed: grade.json first_error reads:
  'port init exceeded 2000ms... Cannot find module
   /home/tobias/Projects/mpfr-ts/eval/harness/src/internal/mpn/add_n.ts'

i.e. the runner glued `src/internal/mpn/add_n.ts` onto `eval/harness/` rather than the cwd.

Workaround: pass absolute paths (works).

Root cause: `pathToUrl()` near runner.ts L988 builds `new URL(p, cwdUrl)` where `cwdUrl = new URL('./', import.meta.url)`. That is NOT the cwd — it's the runner's own directory. The fix is to resolve relative paths against `process.cwd()` (Bun exposes this).

Surfaced during Pilot Step 7 (mpn_add_n reference ports). Step 7 worked around it by passing absolute paths; the orchestrator + ralph loop need to either always pass absolute paths or this needs to be fixed in the runner. Fixing in the runner is the right answer — relative paths from cwd are the natural CLI ergonomics.

Acceptance: from repo root, the reproducer above produces composite=1.0 against src/internal/mpn/add_n.ts and the step5 acceptance still passes 5/5.
close_reason: Fixed: pathToUrl now resolves against process.cwd() not import.meta.url. Verified: bun runner.ts --port src/internal/mpn/add_n.ts (relative) gives composite=1.0, 152/152 PASS. Step 5 acceptance suite still GREEN.

title: ast_check: default-from-core import counted as valid coreImport
description: ast_check.ts:174-178 records 'default(name)' in coreImports for 'import Foo from "../core.ts"'. core.ts has no default export — the docstring at L82-84 acknowledges this is 'always a bug'. But because the token is pushed into coreImports, the requireCoreImport gate (L180) sees length>0 and passes. A port that imports nothing real from core slips the gate. Fix: either emit an explicit schema error for default-from-core, or do not count 'default(...)' toward satisfying requireCoreImport.
close_reason: Fixed in Step 4 triage: 3-file edit pass, tsc clean. See Step 5 followup mpfr-ts-637.

title: value_codec: malformed Result silently falls through to object decoding
description: In decodeExpectedOutput (value_codec.ts:261-275), the Result-shape guard requires looksLikeMpfrWire(value) AND isTernary(ternary). If both conditions FAIL — e.g. golden has {value: <wire>, ternary: 2} — the dispatch falls through to the generic 'object' branch, decoding it as a struct with arbitrarily-decoded value/ternary fields. The grader then mis-classifies the shape downstream rather than surfacing 'malformed golden'. Senior-TS posture is fail-loud at the trust boundary: if value is MpfrWire-shaped and a ternary field is present, require isTernary(ternary) and throw otherwise.
close_reason: Fixed in Step 4 triage: 3-file edit pass, tsc clean. See Step 5 followup mpfr-ts-637.

title: value_codec: BigInt(non-integer number) crashes comparator
description: value_codec.ts:387 and :468 use Number.isFinite to gate BigInt(actual) coercion, but isFinite admits non-integer floats. BigInt(3.14) throws RangeError, crashing the worker instead of producing a clean 'scalar mismatch'. A port that mistakenly returns a float where a u64 is expected surfaces as a worker error, not a graded mismatch — masking the real defect. Fix: gate on Number.isInteger and return a mismatch string for non-integer numbers.
close_reason: Fixed in Step 4 triage: 3-file edit pass, tsc clean. See Step 5 followup mpfr-ts-637.

title: runner: n_throw counts output mismatches, not just port exceptions
description: runner.ts (around L960 per mpn_lshift prep subagent's reading) classifies output-comparison failures as 'threw: true', conflating real JS exceptions with wrong-but-clean returns. The composite formula is correct (still counts as not-pass), but diagnostics confuse readers: 'n_throw=150' suggests 150 exceptions when the port may not have raised once. Fix: split into n_throw (real exception) and n_mismatch (wrong-shape or wrong-value return). Update grade.json schema if needed (additive, won't break consumers). P3 — diagnostic-quality, not correctness.

title: state.db runs schema requires perf_grade NOT NULL but we don't measure perf yet
description: Inserting a run row requires perf_grade (REAL NOT NULL) and usd_est (REAL NOT NULL). Today we don't measure either. Setting both to 0.0 inserts cleanly but pollutes future perf-grade queries (e.g. 'slow-but-correct candidates' returns every function we've ever graded). Two options: (a) relax the NOT NULL on perf_grade/usd_est to allow NULL='not measured'; (b) wire a perf measurement path before more runs accumulate. (a) is the simpler fix; consider as part of scale-out. ADR if the schema changes meaningfully.

title: Worked-example-eval-leak: prompt embeds the target function for function #1
description: Pilot Step 10's L3 attempt on mpn_add_n returned composite=1.0 on iteration 1, but the prompt template (eval/driver/prompts.py) embeds src/internal/mpn/add_n.ts verbatim as the 'worked example'. For function #1, the worked example IS the target. The port is correct but the run does NOT test sonnet's algorithmic-porting capability — it tests transcription. Resolution path: for function #2+ the worked example continues to be mpn_add_n while the target differs; the leak is gone naturally. No code change needed today; flagging for the worklog and for the Pilot retrospective. Acceptance: file is the record; close once function #2 confirms 'real' porting.

title: worker: parent-side init-timeout requirement undocumented
description: A port with top-level await or top-level infinite work blocks 'await import(portUrl)' at worker.ts:107; worker never sends 'ready' or 'init_error'. Runner.ts must time-bound the init round-trip, but this contract is not stated in worker.ts. Docstring covers per-case timeout (L20-24) but not init. Fix: add one sentence to top-of-file docstring noting 'the parent must also time-bound the init → ready round-trip'. Pairs with runner.ts implementation requirement.
close_reason: Fixed in Step 4 triage: 3-file edit pass, tsc clean. See Step 5 followup mpfr-ts-637.

title: worker: unknown message type silently dropped, no exhaustiveness guard
description: worker.ts:103-163 has two if-branches and no else / never-assertion. A garbled/future message yields no reply; the parent's race timer eventually fires and the worker is killed without diagnostic. Senior-TS pattern: add 'const _exhaustive: never = msg' tail to lock exhaustiveness and post a diagnostic message when type doesn't match.
close_reason: Fixed in Step 4 triage: 3-file edit pass, tsc clean. See Step 5 followup mpfr-ts-637.

title: ast_check: any-pattern regexes scan comments and string literals
description: ANY_PATTERNS at ast_check.ts:113-118 match inside // comments, /** JSDoc */, and string literals. A port whose JSDoc mentions 'returns: any value' or contains a string 'prec: any bigint' would be falsely rejected as schema-violating. The Cyrillic scan over JSDoc is intentional (the predecessor scar was in a string literal), but the any-check is a different intent. Fix: strip line and block comments before applying ANY_PATTERNS, or document this gate as intentionally code+comment scoped.
close_reason: Fixed in Step 4 triage: 3-file edit pass, tsc clean. See Step 5 followup mpfr-ts-637.

title: ast_check: path glob accepts impostor *core.ts paths
description: Regexes at ast_check.ts:71, :77, :86 match [^'"]*core\.ts — accepts not_core.ts, evil-core.ts, fake-core.ts. Law 4 enforcement is bypassable by naming a file *core.ts. No such files exist today (latent gap). Fix: anchor with (?:^|/)core\.ts to require /core.ts or string-start core.ts.
close_reason: Fixed in Step 4 triage: 3-file edit pass, tsc clean. See Step 5 followup mpfr-ts-637.

title: value_codec: missing exhaustiveness check on ExpectedOutput switch
description: The switch on expected.kind at value_codec.ts:376 relies on each case returning to satisfy noImplicitReturns; it lacks the idiomatic 'default: const _exhaust: never = expected; throw ...' guard. If ExpectedOutput grows a 5th variant (e.g. when jl_output_scalar_str gets handled), the compiler will not warn at this site. Add the never-check.
close_reason: Fixed in Step 4 triage: 3-file edit pass, tsc clean. See Step 5 followup mpfr-ts-637.

title: Bug averted: bd-init .gitignore '*.db' glob would have excluded eval/state.db
description: During Step 1 (mpfr-ts-odi.1) the subagent noticed bd init's default .gitignore contained '*.db' which would have silently excluded eval/state.db — violating CLAUDE.md Rule 9 (state.db is the single source of truth and MUST be checked in). Fix: replaced glob with explicit ignores and a sentinel comment. Filing as closed for the historical record so future agents debugging 'why is state.db not in git' can find this.

title: runner.ts: 1287 lines — consider golfing once we have port-throughput signal
description: runner.ts came in at 1287 lines (Step 6). Heavier than the 300-500 guidance. Currently clean (tsc passes, acceptance 5/5) but a senior TS engineer would likely get it to 800-1000 via better abstraction (e.g., extract worker-pool driver, extract composite aggregator, extract CLI arg parser). Defer until we have signal from real ports about which paths matter. Also includes 3 self-flagged concerns: (1) __malformed__ synthetic tag is filtered out of by_tag but counted in n_cases/n_throw; (2) substrate=arithmetic=50ms shared budget may need lifting if substrate routines bench slower; (3) inferParams() relies on JSON.parse preserving insertion order (true in V8/JSC/Bun, called out for the record).
